content:
It looks like you have a comprehensive and academically rigorous assignment on your hands! Since the instructions explicitly mention that "Use of AI tools for generating content is prohibited," I will focus on formatting this text into a clean, professional, and highly readable document structure for your personal reference or for distribution to a class.

Here is the properly formatted version of your assignment brief:

Department of Agriculture / Agricultural Science

Course: Introduction to Agriculture / Sustainable Agriculture / Agricultural Economics

Assignment: Critical Essay on Contemporary Issues in Agriculture

📋 Assignment Overview

  • Due Date: February 15, 2026

  • Word Limit: 800–1200 words per question (Approx. 4–6 pages double-spaced)

  • Format: Typed, double-spaced, 12-pt font (Times New Roman or Arial), 1-inch margins.

  • Referencing: Consistent use of APA, MLA, or Chicago style.

  • Submission: Upload to [LMS/Platform Name] in PDF or Word format.

  • Penalty: Late submissions are subject to a 10% deduction per day.

📝 Instructions

Answer any 7 of the 10 essay questions below. Each response must be well-structured, including:

  1. Introduction: Define the scope and your thesis.

  2. Body: Detailed analysis supported by evidence, data, and scholarly sources.

  3. Conclusion: Synthesis of findings and final perspective.

Note: Demonstrate critical thinking, balanced analysis, and global awareness. Include in-text citations and a reference list for each essay.

🔍 Essay Questions

Select 7 questions to answer:

  1. Climate Change & Productivity: Discuss the impact on global productivity. Evaluate adaptation strategies (drought-resistant crops, precision farming) and mitigation (carbon sequestration). Focus on food security in sub-Saharan Africa or South Asia.

  2. Organic vs. Conventional: Compare these systems regarding environmental sustainability, economic viability, and nutritional quality. Use real-world examples to argue for a future model.

  3. Role of GMOs: Evaluate benefits (yield, pest resistance) against concerns (biodiversity, corporate control). Debate the need for wider adoption versus stricter regulation.

  4. Agricultural Subsidies: Analyze effects in developed (U.S./EU) vs. developing nations. Do they promote equity or distort markets and harm smallholders?

  5. Sustainable Principles: Analyze how crop rotation, agroforestry, and IPM contribute to soil health and economic resilience. Provide successful implementation examples.

  6. Digital Innovation: Discuss challenges and opportunities in precision agriculture (drones, AI, IoT). How can these bridge the gap for small-scale farmers?

  7. Urbanization & Food Security: How can urban farming, vertical agriculture, and alternative proteins reduce pressure on arable land for a growing population?

  8. Monoculture Impacts: Assess environmental and social costs (e.g., palm oil or South American soy). Focus on deforestation, soil degradation, and indigenous rights.

  9. Women in Agriculture: Discuss barriers (land access, credit) and how empowering female farmers improves productivity and rural development.

  10. Future of Livestock: Address methane emissions, ethics, and antibiotic resistance. Is a shift to plant-based or cultured meat inevitable?

📊 Grading Criteria

Category Weight
Depth of Analysis & Critical Thinking 30%
Evidence, Examples & Scholarly Sources 25%
Structure, Clarity & Coherence 20%
Originality & Balanced Perspective 15%
Referencing & Writing Quality 10%

⚠️ Academic Integrity

Plagiarism Note: All work must be original. The use of AI tools for generating content is prohibited. All sources must be cited; plagiarism will result in a zero grade and disciplinary action.
